patterns.title: Recursive correction parenting
patterns.description: Correction history is encoded as progressively deeper product hierarchy instead of staying attached to a stable outcome.

patterns.title: Outcome-lineage retry reset
patterns.description: A replacement task identity resets correction limits while pursuing the same unresolved outcome or finding ancestry.

patterns.title: Release verification in ordinary task work
patterns.description: An ordinary implementation or correction repeatedly runs broad release-grade checks it does not own.

patterns.title: Unreviewed dependency represented as reviewed
patterns.description: A worker consumes an unreviewed commit through reviewed-dependency authority instead of an explicit speculative contract.

patterns.title: Excessive controller polling
patterns.description: The controller repeatedly polls healthy workers instead of using completion signals and phase overrun thresholds.

patterns.title: Episodic memory capture
patterns.description: A procedural milestone with no future decision value is stored as durable memory.

patterns.title: Editor opened for finished artifact
patterns.description: The workflow opens a completed product contract, spec, or plan in the user's editor without an explicit request.

patterns.title: Long interval without stable slice closure
patterns.description: Implementation and correction activity continues for a long interval without closing a stable planned slice or outcome.
